title: Platform Workers CPF Transition Support (PCTS)
text:
Platform Workers CPF Transition Support (PCTS) - SupportGoWhere
Platform Workers CPF Transition Support (PCTS)
Share link
From 2025 to 2028, the Platform Workers CPF Transition Support (PCTS) scheme provides monthly cash payments to support eligible lower-income platform workers who make increased CPF Ordinary and Special/Retirement Account contributions.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Monthly cash payments from 2025 to 2028 to support lower-income platform workers who see an increase in their share of Ordinary and Special/Retirement Account contributions
Lower-income platform workers who qualify for PCTS will automatically receive the payments; there is no need to apply
Payments credited directly to PayNow NRIC-linked bank accounts, registered bank accounts or via GovCash
Collapse all sections
About this support

Monthly cash payments from 2025 to 2028 to offset part of the year-on-year increase in the platform worker’s share of CPF Ordinary and Special/Retirement Account contributions for eligible lower-income platform workers.

An online PCTS calculator is available to estimate how much you can receive.

Who is this for?

Singapore Citizen only

Born in 1995 or later, or born before 1995 and opted in to increase CPF contributions;

Net income from platform work (i.e. gross earnings minus the Fixed Expense Deduction Amount) and other employment sources does not exceed $3,000 per month;

Must have an increase in platform worker’s own share of CPF contributions to the Ordinary and Special/Retirement Account compared to the previous year

How to apply?

Assessment for PCTS is automatic, no application is required.

Where can I find help?

View more details

CPFB Hotline: 1800 227 1188

To avoid waiting on the line, you may book an appointment here.

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit your nearest branch

Note: By appointment only

Scheme last updated 10 Mar 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
